instruction:
Let $y_{1}=2 x-\mathrm{e}^{x}, y_{2}=2 x+\mathrm{e}^{x}$ be two particular solutions for the differential equation $y^{\prime}+a \left( x \right) y=b \left( x \right)$, then () is right.

Options:
A. $$a \left( x \right)=-1, b \left( x \right)=2 x-\mathrm{e}^{x}$$
B. $$a \left( x \right)=1, b \left( x \right)=2+2 \mathrm{e}^{x}$$
C. $$a \left( x \right)=1, b \left( x \right)=2+2 x$$
D. $$a \left( x \right)=-1, b \left( x \right)=2+2 x$$
E. $$a \left( x \right)=-1, b \left( x \right)=2+2 \mathrm{e}^{x}$$
F. $$a \left( x \right)=1, b \left( x \right)=2-2 x$$
G. $$a \left( x \right)=-1, b \left( x \right)=2-2 \mathrm{e}^{2x}$$
H. $$a \left( x \right)=-1, b \left( x \right)=2-2 \mathrm{e}^{x}$$
I. $$a \left( x \right)=-1, b \left( x \right)=2-2 x$$
J. $$a \left( x \right)=-1, b \left( x \right)=\mathrm{e}^{x}-2 x$$
